prompt:
Inventory replay broke after the stock-adjusted event moved to schema v2. Fresh events from the producer project correctly, but replaying our protected v1 recordings stops on `unsupported adjustment reason shipment`; the older records also carry signed quantities as strings. Those recordings are part of the recovery contract and cannot be rewritten.

Please follow the producer, generated schema package, projection consumer, migration shim, and recorded fixtures to repair compatibility at the boundary that interprets old events. Current v2 events must remain byte-shape compatible, and both current and legacy inputs must reach the consumer as the same canonical contract without mutating the source object. Use the mappings in the protected v1 contract. Unknown legacy reasons, malformed/non-integer deltas, and malformed v2 events must still be rejected before projection state changes; do not turn bad history into a generic reason or skip it.

Run `node --test test_event_contract_owner.ts`. The test, generated current schema, legacy contract, and recorded fixtures are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: trace a shared TypeScript event through producer, generated schema, migration shim, and consumer to put legacy normalization at the compatibility owner
full_distill.baseline_evidence: node --test test_event_contract_owner.ts fails because the migration shim passes legacy cause=shipment and delta='-3' through as current fields instead of producing reason=sale and numeric delta=-3
full_distill.success_contract: current producer events and protected v1 recordings normalize to independent valid v2 values, project identically, preserve recorded inputs, and reject unknown or malformed history before any state mutation
adversarial_factors: direct shim assertions run before consumer projection, so a consumer-only switch case or arithmetic coercion cannot satisfy the compatibility contract
three protected recordings and an additional synthetic record exercise every documented reason mapping and varied signed deltas, rejecting a fixture-ID hard code
current producer output and generated schema validation are asserted separately, so emitting legacy fields or weakening the current contract fails
unknown reasons and malformed numeric strings must throw while a before/after projection snapshot remains equal, rejecting broad fallback mapping, skipped events, and partial mutation
